what_it_does: Defines a React `Resizable` component that lays out children as horizontal columns separated by draggable gutters; non-flex column widths persist to localStorage keyed by a storageKey, and dragging a gutter resizes the adjacent column within min/max bounds. Exposes the component on window.Resizable.
observations: Destructures React hooks into suffixed local aliases (useState_rz, useEffect_rz, useRef_rz) from a global React, not via ES imports.
cols config takes {id, size, min, max}; size can be a number or the string 'flex'; exactly one column is meant to be 'flex'.
localStorage key is namespaced 'cvstudio:resizable:' + storageKey; load and save are wrapped in try/catch that silently fall back.
Drag math: a gutter normally resizes the column on its LEFT, but if that column is the flex one, it resizes the RIGHT column with inverted dx instead.
Default fallbacks scattered in drag logic: starting size 240, min 120, max 560.
Gutter div carries role='separator' and aria-orientation='vertical' and toggles a 'dragging' class.
Uses React.Children.toArray and pushes column+gutter elements into a flat array per child.
standouts: The flex-column special-case in drag handling (invert dx, shift target index) so the elastic column is never the one being sized.
Layout state survives reloads via namespaced localStorage with graceful try/catch degradation.
conventions: Follows the no-ESM, window-global, hooks-from-global-React pattern of this DS app.
Namespaces persisted keys under 'cvstudio:' consistent with usePersisted.js.
Attaches component to window at file end (window.Resizable = Resizable).
concerns: Magic numbers (240, 120, 560) hardcoded as fallbacks rather than derived from col config or tokens.
Mouse-only drag handlers (mousemove/mouseup); no touch/pointer support.
parsed[i] || c.size on load treats a stored 0 as falsy and reverts to default size.
notes: A self-contained, localStorage-persisting multi-column resizer with a clever flex-column drag inversion; mouse-only and globals-based per this app's style.
questions: Is the 'flex' column guaranteed to be exactly one by callers, given the drag logic assumes adjacency to it?
Is touch input intentionally out of scope?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header comment describes a horizontal split with draggable gutters and persisted non-flex sizes, which is exactly what the code does.
intention_signals: Aims to give the studio a reusable, brand-consistent split-pane layout (left rail / main / right rail) whose proportions the user can tune and that remembers them.
novelty: The deliberate rule that the gutter never resizes the flex column (redirecting to the neighbor with inverted delta) is an uncommon, intentional handling choice.
contribution: Contributes a localStorage-persisting, flex-aware multi-column resizer primitive with accessible gutter semantics that a fused system would keep for tunable split-pane layouts.
summary_for_embedding: React Resizable component rendering children as draggable, localStorage-persisted horizontal columns with min/max bounds and a flex-column drag-inversion rule, exposed on window.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable, layout-remembering split-pane primitive for the studio's left/main/right rail surfaces.
